message:
feat(actor): add ActorContext with supervision and capability extension

Extends the actor system with a rich context type that provides actors with
supervision capabilities and seamless access to the runtime context:

New types:
- `ActorContext<M>`: Actor-specific capability extension wrapping `Cx`
  - Self-reference via `self_ref()` for tell-to-self patterns
  - Child management via `children()` for supervision trees
  - Parent reference for failure escalation
  - Stopping flag for graceful shutdown coordination
  - Full `Deref` to `Cx` for transparent capability access

- `MailboxConfig`: Configuration for actor mailbox behavior
  - Configurable capacity (default: 1024 messages)
  - Backpressure mode (block senders vs drop oldest)

- `SupervisorMessage`: Child lifecycle event notifications
  - `ChildFailed { child_id, reason }` for failure propagation
  - `ChildStopped { child_id }` for normal termination

Design follows the capability pattern: actors receive a context that grants
them specific powers (self-messaging, child spawning) while inheriting all
`Cx` capabilities (budget checking, cancellation, tracing, obligations).

This lays groundwork for supervision strategies (one-for-one, one-for-all)
and enables actors to participate fully in the structured concurrency model.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
